meta.title: RC-505mk2 FX Rack Guide
meta.subtitle: Signal chains, FX racks & workflows for real-world looping

racks.title: Tight Acoustic Percussion Rack
racks.description: Designed for hand percussion, cajon, or tapping. The chain tightens transients, adds body, and places the sound in a controlled space. Emphasizes punch without boominess.

tips.title: Performance Technique
tips.text: Record your base groove with Slot A–C on, Slot D (reverb) at a low level. On playback, engage the Filter Track FX and sweep it for builds. Hit Beat Repeat at transitions for glitchy fills. Turn on Lo-Fi briefly to add texture to a breakdown, then disengage for the drop.

tips.title: Input Comp & Noise Suppress
tips.text: The RC-505mk2 has dedicated Input Compressor and Noise Suppress controls separate from the FX slots. Configure these at MENU > INPUT: set NS THRESHOLD (30-45 for percussion), and INPUT COMP to taste. These work alongside your FX rack — use Input Comp for basic level control and DYNAMICS in an FX slot for creative compression.

racks.title: Electronic Percussion Processing Rack
racks.description: Transforms acoustic percussion input into hard-hitting electronic drum textures. Uses aggressive compression, bitcrushing, and resonant filtering to create machine-like percussion from organic sources.

tips.title: Layering Technique
tips.text: Record your crushed electronic percussion on Track 1 at full level. Then switch Input FX to the Acoustic Percussion rack (toggle Slot A off) and layer a clean percussion loop on Track 2 at ~60% volume. The blend of clean + crushed creates depth and punch that neither achieves alone.

racks.title: Clean Beatbox Foundation
racks.description: Optimized for clear, punchy vocal percussion. Manages proximity effect and plosives while enhancing the natural snap and thud of beatbox sounds. The chain keeps things tight and defined.

tips.title: Noise Suppress at Input Level
tips.text: Slot A in this rack uses DYNAMICS for gating. For even better noise control, enable Input-level Noise Suppress at MENU > INPUT > NS THRESHOLD (set around 30-40 for beatbox). This works alongside your FX slots for a tighter signal before the chain begins.

racks.title: Synth Beatbox / Robo-Percussion
racks.description: Transforms vocal percussion into synthetic, electronic-sounding hits. Uses the built-in SYNTH effect to reshape mouth sounds into something that sounds closer to a drum machine than a human.

tips.title: Sound Switching
tips.text: Record your synth-processed beatbox on Track 1. Then toggle Input FX A (SYNTH) off mid-set — now your mic captures clean vocals for the next track. Toggle it back on for the next drum layer. Use CTL1/CTL2 assigns to map this toggle for one-button switching between beatbox and vocal modes.

racks.title: Autotune Vocals (Vocoder + MIDI)
racks.description: Creates the hard-tuned, robotic vocal effect popular in modern hip hop and pop. The Vocoder locks pitch to defined intervals while compression and EQ keep the vocal present and polished.

tips.title: Vocoder Setup Required
tips.text: The VOCODER effect requires a carrier signal. Connect a MIDI keyboard's audio output to the RC-505mk2 INST1 input, then set CARRIER to INST1-L. Set MIDI VOICE channel (MENU > MIDI > VOICE CH) to match your keyboard. Play and hold notes on the keyboard while singing — the vocoder locks your voice to those pitches. Without a carrier audio source, the vocoder will not produce sound. For simpler pitch effects without external gear, try ROBOT, ELECTRIC, or OSC VOC instead.

racks.title: Vocal Harmony / Octave Stack
racks.description: Creates instant vocal harmonies using the Octave and Transpose effects. Great for building vocal stacks on the fly — record each harmony to a different track for full control.

tips.title: Multi-Pass Harmony Technique
tips.text:
Pass 1: Record your lead vocal with Octave OFF, Transpose OFF → Track 1.
Pass 2: Enable Transpose at +5 semi, record harmony → Track 2.
Pass 3: Shift to +7 semi, record → Track 3.
Pass 4: Enable Octave -1OCT for bass vocal → Track 4.
Now you have a full choir-style stack with individual track volume control.

racks.title: Warm Radio Vocal
racks.description: A polished, present vocal sound — compression for consistency, EQ for warmth and presence, gentle reverb for depth. Suitable for clean singing loops.

racks.title: Ambient / Ethereal Vocal Wash
racks.description: Creates atmospheric, floating vocal textures — perfect for ambient pads built from voice. Heavy reverb and delay with pitch shifting for an otherworldly quality.

tips.title: Vocal Pad Building
tips.text: Sing a sustained note or chord tone into this rack and loop it on a track set to Overdub mode. Each pass layers another shifted, delayed, reverbed voice — within 3–4 passes you have a lush, evolving vocal pad. Set the track's Play Mode to MULTI for seamless looping.

racks.title: Aggressive Vocal FX (Distorted / Glitch)
racks.description: Harsh, distorted vocal processing for aggressive genres. Transforms clean vocals into gritty, processed textures for breakdowns, drops, and heavy sections.

tips.title: Gain Staging Note
tips.text: When using PREAMP on vocals, the gain structure is very different from actual guitar input. Lower your input level to ~60% before engaging this effect, or you'll get uncontrolled clipping. Adjust the GAIN parameter to taste — on vocals, even GAIN at 50 sounds heavily distorted.

racks.title: Clean / Neo-Soul / Lo-Fi Guitar
racks.description: Warm, round clean guitar tone — the kind used in lo-fi beats, neo-soul jams, and chill production. Gentle chorus for width, compression for evenness.

racks.title: Distorted / Rock / Metal Guitar
racks.description: High-gain guitar processing for rock and metal — drive, tight low-end, and presence. Uses PREAMP's heavier amp models with sculpted EQ for modern tones.

racks.title: Ambient / Post-Rock Guitar
racks.description: Swelling, washy guitar textures for ambient soundscapes. Uses Slow Gear for volume swells, with heavy delay and reverb to create evolving, cinematic layers.

tips.title: Ambient Building
tips.text: Set a track to Overdub mode. Play a chord, let the Slow Gear swell it in, and the delay/reverb will smear it into a wash. Each overdub pass adds another layer. After 3–4 passes, stop recording but keep playing back — you now have a rich ambient bed. Add Track FX Filter (LPF) and slowly close it for a fading, cinematic effect.

racks.title: Clean Keys / Electric Piano
racks.description: For Rhodes, Wurlitzer, or clean synth patches. Adds warmth, subtle motion, and space without coloring the keyboard's native tone too heavily.

racks.title: Synth Lead / Bass Processing
racks.description: For external synths and bass synths — adds movement, grit, and rhythmic interest using the RC-505mk2's own FX chain on top of your synth's sound.

racks.title: Robot Voice / Daft Punk Style
racks.description: Classic robotic vocal effect — locks pitch to a fixed note for that iconic Daft Punk sound. No MIDI keyboard required. Change the NOTE parameter between takes to create melodies.

tips.title: Melody Building
tips.text: ROBOT locks your voice to a fixed pitch set by the NOTE parameter. Change NOTE between takes to create melodies — C for verse, E for pre-chorus, G for chorus. Use CTL assigns to switch NOTE values on the fly. For chords, record each note to a separate track with different NOTE values.

racks.title: Electric Pitch Correction (No MIDI)
racks.description: Subtle to aggressive pitch correction using ELECTRIC effect. Set SCALE to your song's key for automatic pitch snapping. No external gear required — great T-Pain alternative without MIDI.

tips.title: Speed Controls the Vibe
tips.text: SPEED controls how aggressively the pitch snaps. Low values (2-3) give smooth, natural correction — your voice glides to the correct note. Higher values (7-10) create the hard, robotic T-Pain snap. SCALE must match your song's key or the correction will sound wrong. STABILITY controls how easily pitch changes — higher values resist pitch changes more.

racks.title: OSC Vocoder (No MIDI Required)
racks.description: Creates a vocoder sound using a built-in oscillator as the carrier — no MIDI keyboard needed. Your voice shapes the oscillator's tone for that classic talk-box/vocoder texture.

tips.title: Waveform Selection
tips.text: Change the CARRIER waveform for different textures: SAW is bright and buzzy (classic vocoder), VINTAGE SAW is warmer, SQUARE is hollow and synthetic, RECT is nasal. OCTAVE shifts the oscillator pitch — try -1OCT for a deep vocoder bass or +1OCT for a chipmunk effect. Requires MIDI keyboard for pitch control.

racks.title: Lo-Fi / Vintage Vocal
racks.description: Authentic vintage vocal character — bitcrushing combined with tape echo creates the sound of recording on a worn cassette deck. Perfect for lo-fi hip hop vocal samples.

tips.title: Vintage Vibe
tips.text: LO-FI bitcrushing + TAPE ECHO creates an authentic vintage/cassette feel. The LOW CUT and HIGH CUT on the reverb narrow the frequency range for that old recording sound. Boost TAPE ECHO BASS for a warmer tone, or increase LO-FI BITDEPTH degradation (lower number = more crushed).

racks.title: Telephone / Radio Voice
racks.description: Narrow-band vocal processing that mimics a telephone line or old radio broadcast. Great for breakdowns, intros, and dramatic contrast against clean vocals.

tips.title: Dramatic Contrast
tips.text: Layer this as a breakdown or bridge vocal on a separate track. Switch to this rack mid-performance for dramatic contrast against your clean vocal — the sudden narrow bandwidth is an ear-catching effect. Try singing the first line of a chorus through this rack, then switching to a full-range rack for the rest.

racks.title: Vocal Chop / Glitch Machine
racks.description: Transforms sustained vocals into rhythmic glitch patterns. Granular delay + pattern slicer chops and stutters your voice into something entirely new.

tips.title: Glitch Technique
tips.text: Sing sustained notes or short phrases into this rack. The Granular Delay + Pattern Slicer combination chops and stutters your voice into rhythmic glitch patterns. Record the result, then overdub clean vocals on top for a textured contrast. Try different PATTERN values (P01-P20) for different rhythmic feels.

racks.title: Whisper to Scream (Extreme Dynamics)
racks.description: Heavy limiting makes whispers nearly as loud as shouts, while distortion adds natural grit that increases with input level. Whispers stay clean, loud singing breaks up.

tips.title: Dynamics Control
tips.text: HARD LIM with high DYNAMICS compresses whispers to be nearly as loud as shouts. The DIST (VOCAL type) adds grit that increases with input level — whispers stay clean, louder singing breaks up naturally. Great for building intensity within a single take. Watch your monitoring levels — the limiting makes everything loud.

racks.title: Choir Builder / Vocal Pad
racks.description: Builds lush vocal pads and choir textures through overdub layering. Transpose + chorus + long reverb glues multiple vocal passes into a cohesive pad.

tips.title: Multi-Pass Choir
tips.text: Use Overdub mode to stack vocal layers. Pass 1: Sing root notes with TRANS at +7 (perfect 5th). Pass 2: Change TRANS to +5 (major 3rd). Pass 3: Change TRANS to +12 (octave). Pass 4: Set TRANS to 0 and sing the root. Each pass adds another harmony layer. The heavy chorus + long reverb glues layers into a pad-like wash.

racks.title: Double-Tracked Vocals
racks.description: Creates the illusion of two voices singing together using a very short modulated delay. Thickens any vocal without sounding obviously processed.

tips.title: Natural Doubling
tips.text: MOD DELAY with very short TIME (25-35ms) and single repeat creates a natural doubling effect — like two voices singing slightly out of sync. STEREO ENHANCE widens the image. This thickens any vocal without sounding obviously effected. Works on lead vocals, harmonies, and even spoken word.

racks.title: Voice → Sub-Bass / 808 Kick
racks.description: Transforms vocal bass sounds into deep sub-bass and 808-style kicks using G2B and Octave effects. The foundation of any beatbox bass line.

tips.title: 808 Kick Technique
tips.text: TECHNIQUE: Hum a low 'mmm' sound from your chest, not your throat. Place the mic close to (or touching) your throat for maximum bass pickup. For a punchy 808 kick, make a sharp 'BOH' sound using your diaphragm — keep it short and explosive. G2B tracks your pitch and generates bass. For sustained sub-bass, hold a steady low hum and let G2B + OCTAVE generate the sub frequencies.

racks.title: Voice → Electric Bass Line
racks.description: Creates playable electric bass tones from your voice using dual octave generation and filtered warmth. Sing melodies and the RC-505mk2 turns them into bass lines.

tips.title: Bass Line Technique
tips.text: TECHNIQUE: Sing a sustained 'doo' or 'daa' at a comfortable mid-range pitch. The OCTAVE effect adds both -1 and -2 octave harmonics below your voice. Articulate each note cleanly — sloppy transitions between notes create pitch tracking artifacts. Use your tongue ('da-da-da') for a more plucked/fingered bass feel. For a slap bass sound, use a sharp 'Pa' or 'Ta' attack.

racks.title: Voice → Trumpet / Brass
racks.description: Mimics brass instrument tone by adding harmonic overtones to lip buzzing. Distortion adds the resonant quality of a brass bell while EQ shapes the characteristic presence peak.

tips.title: Brass Sound Technique
tips.text: TECHNIQUE: Buzz your lips together like blowing a raspberry, but with more control and steady pitch. Press the mic directly against your lips to capture the buzz. Shape notes by changing the tightness of your lip buzz — tighter = higher pitch. The DIST (BOOST) adds harmonic overtones that mimic brass resonance, while the EQ boosts the 2-3 kHz presence range where brass instruments naturally project.

racks.title: Voice → Violin / Strings
racks.description: Creates bowed string instrument textures from hummed notes. Slow Gear provides natural volume swells like bow strokes while chorus and reverb add ensemble width.

tips.title: String Sound Technique
tips.text: TECHNIQUE: Hum a sustained 'eee' through your nose with slight vibrato. The nasal resonance mimics the thin, harmonic-rich quality of bowed strings. SLOW GEAR creates natural volume swells like a bow stroke. Stack multiple passes on Overdub mode — change TRANS between passes (+5, +7, +12) to build a full string section. Keep each note long and steady for the most realistic result.

racks.title: Dubstep Wobble Bass from Voice
racks.description: Transforms lip bass into a classic dubstep wobble using the SYNTH effect with a resonant sweeping filter. Assign LPF cutoff to a knob for live wobble control.

tips.title: Wobble Bass Technique
tips.text: TECHNIQUE: Make a sustained lip bass or throat bass — a continuous 'brrr' vibration with your lips barely touching. The SYNTH reshapes this into a synth tone, the LPF with high RESONANCE creates the wobble sweep. Assign LPF CUTOFF to a CTL knob and sweep it manually in rhythm for a live wobble. For automated wobble, lower the LPF RATE value.

racks.title: Voice → EDM Synth Lead
racks.description: Transforms clean singing into synthesizer lead tones. The SYNTH effect tracks your pitch while overdrive and delay add character and dimension.

tips.title: Synth Lead Technique
tips.text: TECHNIQUE: Sing or hum clean, sustained notes. The SYNTH effect tracks your pitch and generates a synthesizer tone. Articulate precisely — each note should start clean. For a plucked synth sound, use short 'da' or 'ta' syllables. For sustained leads, use 'ooo' with steady breath support. Higher FREQUENCY values make the synth brighter; lower values make it darker.

racks.title: Vocal Drum Machine
racks.description: Heavy bitcrushing and compression transform organic mouth percussion into tight, digital drum machine hits. Gate reverb adds the punchy 80s drum machine ambience.

tips.title: Drum Machine Technique
tips.text: TECHNIQUE: Use classic beatbox sounds: 'B' for kick (sharp lip pop from diaphragm), 'Pf' for snare (push air between lips), 'Ts' for hi-hat (tongue against teeth), 'K' for rimshot (tongue click). The heavy bitcrushing + compression transforms organic mouth sounds into tight digital drum machine hits. GATE REVERB adds the punchy, cut-off ambience of an 80s drum machine.

racks.title: Expressive Dubstep / Filth Bass
racks.description: Aggressive, expressive bass sounds using PREAMP distortion with resonant filtering. The 'talking bass' sound used in heavy dubstep — your jaw movements control the formant sweep.

tips.title: Filth Bass Technique
tips.text: TECHNIQUE: Create a 'yoi-yoi' sound by opening and closing your jaw while sustaining a vocal fry or throat bass. The jaw movement naturally sweeps formant frequencies, creating the expressive dubstep 'talking bass' sound. PREAMP with heavy GAIN adds aggressive distortion, BPF shapes the midrange character, and OCTAVE thickens it an octave down. For extra grit, try a lip oscillation bass instead.

racks.title: Jazz / Smooth Clean
racks.description: Classic warm jazz guitar tone — very low gain JC-120 amp with rolled-off treble. The CND87 condenser mic model adds a smooth, hi-fi quality perfect for jazz chord work.

tips.title: Jazz Tone Tips
tips.text: Keep GAIN very low (5-10) and roll off the treble for a warm, round tone. The JC-120 is the classic clean jazz amp. The CND87 mic model adds a slightly scooped, hi-fi quality. For a warmer sound, try NATURAL CLEAN amp type. Roll your guitar's tone knob down to about 5-6 for the classic jazz neck pickup tone.

racks.title: Indie / Jangle Clean
racks.description: Bright, chimey clean tone with subtle modulation — the jangly guitar sound of indie rock. COMBO amp with higher treble and presence for that sparkling top end.

tips.title: Jangle Tone
tips.text: COMBO amp with moderate treble and presence gives that chimey, jangly tone characteristic of bands like The Smiths and R.E.M. The subtle phaser adds movement without being obvious. Keep delay and reverb modest — this should sound like a bright, live room.

racks.title: Blues / Classic Crunch
racks.description: Classic blues breakup tone — clean when you play softly, gritty when you dig in. CRUNCH amp at moderate gain preserves the touch sensitivity that makes blues expressive.

tips.title: Blues Touch
tips.text: CRUNCH amp at moderate GAIN gives classic blues breakup — clean when you play softly, gritty when you dig in. This dynamic response is what makes blues tone expressive. Keep the DYNAMICS light to preserve touch sensitivity. Roll your guitar volume knob for instant clean-to-crunch transitions.

racks.title: Punk / Garage Rock
racks.description: Tight, aggressive crunch with short, dry reverb. STACK CRUNCH provides a saturated mid-range grind with added OD distortion for that raw garage energy.

tips.title: Garage Energy
tips.text: STACK CRUNCH gives a tight, aggressive mid-range crunch. Adding the OD distortion on top pushes it into garage territory. Keep reverb very short and dry — punk/garage tone is upfront and in-your-face. Power chord riffs sound best with this chain.

racks.title: Djent / Modern Metal
racks.description: Ultra-tight modern metal tone with the classic djent mid-scoop at 200 Hz. CORE METAL amp with high T-COMP for percussive palm-muted playing. Gate reverb replaces a noise gate.

tips.title: Djent Tone
tips.text: The tight GATE REVERB replaces the noise gate that metal players need — it cuts reverb tails sharply for palm-muted clarity. The EQ scoops low-mids at 200 Hz (the classic djent scoop) while boosting presence. T-COMP at 8 tightens the amp's response for percussive playing. DYN421 mic model at 3cm gives maximum attack definition.

racks.title: Shoegaze / Wall of Sound
racks.description: Massive, enveloping guitar wash — fuzz distortion through reverse delay and ocean-deep reverb. Layer on overdub mode for the classic shoegaze wall of sound.

tips.title: Wall of Sound
tips.text: Layer on overdub mode — each pass adds more density to the wall of sound. The REVERSE DELAY creates signature backwards-sounding swells. FUZZ with dark TONE keeps things thick but not harsh. This rack lives at high volumes. For even more density, add Track FX FLANGER at a slow rate.

racks.title: Acoustic Guitar Enhancement
racks.description: Cleans up common acoustic pickup issues — cuts the 250 Hz boxiness, boosts sparkle, and adds subtle width. The ACOUSTIC dynamics type is designed specifically for acoustic guitar.

tips.title: Acoustic Pickup Fix
tips.text: The EQ cuts the 250 Hz boxiness common with acoustic pickups and boosts the 5 kHz sparkle. DYNAMICS set to ACOUSTIC is specifically designed for acoustic guitar dynamics. Very subtle CHORUS adds width without sounding obviously processed. Works with both piezo and magnetic pickups.

racks.title: Acoustic Fingerstyle / Percussive
racks.description: Optimized for percussive fingerstyle players who tap, slap, and pluck. Higher dynamics capture both quiet fingerpicked notes and percussive body hits with equal clarity.

tips.title: Percussive Playing
tips.text: Higher DYNAMICS value captures both the quiet fingerpicked notes and the percussive body hits. The EQ boosts both the bass thump (body percussion) and the treble sparkle (harmonics and picks). STEREO ENHANCE widens the stereo image for a more immersive sound. Great for Andy McKee / Mike Dawes style playing.

racks.title: DJ Filter Sweep
racks.description: Classic DJ-style filter manipulation for recorded loops. HPF sweep strips lows for buildups, LPF sweep removes highs for breakdowns. Delay adds depth to filtered signal. Pattern slicer creates rhythmic gating. Ideal for live performance transitions.

tips.title: Build & Drop
tips.text: Engage HPF and slowly sweep CUTOFF from 0 to 80+ to strip lows during a build. At the drop, disengage HPF. For breakdowns, engage LPF and sweep CUTOFF down from 100. Combine with the delay for filtered echo trails.

racks.title: Beat Mangler
racks.description: Aggressive beat manipulation rack for performable glitch and stutter effects on recorded loops. Beat scatter rearranges slices, beat repeat creates fills, roll adds stutter, and the lo-fi degrades the signal for texture. Perfect for DJ transitions and drops.

tips.title: Glitch Fill
tips.text: Bank A is for rhythmic manipulation: engage Scatter for slice rearrangement, Roll for rapid stutter fills. Bank B is for dramatic moments: Repeat for build fills, Twist for risers, Warp for tape-stop effects, Freeze for sustain drones. Switch banks mid-performance for variety.

tips.title: Special TFX Rule
tips.text: BEAT SCATTER, BEAT REPEAT, BEAT SHIFT, and VINYL FLICK can only go in Slot A of each bank — one per bank. This rack puts SCATTER in Bank A Slot A and REPEAT in Bank B Slot A.

racks.title: Ambient Loop Wash
racks.description: Transform any recorded loop into ambient textures. Reverse reverb creates ethereal swells, chorus adds shimmer, HPF removes mud, and tremolo adds movement. Works beautifully on vocals, guitar, or any melodic content.

tips.title: Ambient Layering
tips.text: Record a simple vocal phrase or guitar chord. Engage the full TFX chain to transform it into a lush ambient pad. Use the HPF cutoff to thin or thicken the texture. Adjust tremolo rate for faster or slower pulsing. Great for building atmosphere underneath other loop layers.

racks.title: Vinyl DJ Toolkit
racks.description: Turntablist-inspired track manipulation. Vinyl flick for scratch stops, isolator for DJ-style band kills, phaser for sweeps, and panning delay for spatial fills. Bank B adds beat shift and flanger for more variety.

tips.title: Turntablist Workflow
tips.text: Bank A: Use Vinyl Flick for dramatic stops, Isolator to kill frequency bands (change BAND param to LOW/MIDDLE/HIGH), Phaser for sweeping transitions, and Panning Delay for spatial fills. Bank B: Beat Shift for time-displaced glitches, Flanger for jet sweeps, Auto Pan for stereo movement.

genreTemplates.title: Hip Hop / Boom Bap Looping Session
genreTemplates.description: 5-track layout: beatbox drums → bass vocal → melody/sample → vocal verse → vocal hook. Designed for building a full beat with just a mic.
genreTemplates.performanceTip: Start with Track 1 — lay your drum pattern (2–4 bars). Switch Input FX and drop a bass line on Track 2. Build up the melody on Track 3 with everything playing. For the verse, mute tracks 3 and 5, engage Track 4 and spit bars over tracks 1+2. Unmute everything for the hook on Track 5.

genreTemplates.title: EDM / House Build & Drop
genreTemplates.description: Structured for the classic build → drop format. Tracks dedicated to kick, bass, lead, pads, and a vocal chop layer.
genreTemplates.performanceTip: Build: Start all 5 tracks playing. Engage Track FX LPF on Track 1 and sweep the CUTOFF up. Simultaneously sweep Track 3's LPF CUTOFF up. Engage BEAT REPEAT on Track 5 at FORWARD mode for a stuttering riser. Drop: At the peak, simultaneously disengage ALL Track FX with a single assigned button (CTL1 → ALL TRK FX OFF).

genreTemplates.title: Dubstep / Bass Music
genreTemplates.description: Heavy sub-bass focus, aggressive processing, and performable wobble effects using Track FX automation.
genreTemplates.performanceTip: Assign the Track FX LPF CUTOFF on Track 3 to a knob (CTRL knob). Record a sustained bass note, then on playback, manually sweep the knob back and forth in rhythm for a classic dubstep wobble bass.

genreTemplates.title: Modern Rock / Alternative
genreTemplates.description: Guitar-centric looping with dedicated tracks for rhythm, lead, bass, vocals, and ambient textures.
genreTemplates.performanceTip: Build your rhythm guitar foundation on Track 1, then layer bass on Track 3. Add percussion on Track 4, lead on Track 2, and finish with vocals on Track 5.

genreTemplates.title: Synthwave / Retrowave
genreTemplates.description: 80s-inspired electronic production — arpeggiated synths, gated drums, lush pads, and vocoded vocals.
genreTemplates.performanceTip: Start with the drum machine groove, layer the bass synth, then add the arp. The pad goes on Track 4 for atmosphere. Finish with vocoded vocals for that classic 80s feel.

vocoderGuide.title: Using the Vocoder & Pitch Effects
vocoderGuide.intro: The RC-505mk2 offers several pitch-related effects. Understanding which to use — and their requirements — is key.

effects.name: VOCODER
effects.requirement: Requires MIDI keyboard connected to MIDI IN
effects.setup: Set MIDI VOICE channel at MENU > MIDI > VOICE CH. Use CARRIER, TONE, ATTACK, MOD SENS, BALANCE, CARRIER THRU params. Play notes on keyboard while singing. Voice is locked to played pitches.
effects.bestFor: T-Pain / auto-tune vocal style, vocoded pads, robotic singing

effects.name: OSC VOC
effects.requirement: No external gear needed — uses built-in oscillator as carrier
effects.setup: Select OSC VOC in any Input FX slot. Adjust CARRIER (SAW, VINTAGE SAW, etc.), TONE, ATTACK, OCTAVE, MOD SENS, RELEASE, and BALANCE params.
effects.bestFor: Quick robotic/synthetic vocal texture without MIDI setup

effects.name: ROBOT
effects.setup: Select ROBOT in any Input FX slot. Adjust NOTE, FORMANT, and MODE params.
effects.bestFor: Classic robotic voice effect (Daft Punk style)

effects.name: ELECTRIC
effects.setup: Select ELECTRIC in any Input FX slot. Adjust SHIFT, FORMANT, SPEED, STABILITY, and SCALE params.
effects.bestFor: Electronic voice modulation, pitch shifting on the fly

effects.name: TRANSPOSE
effects.setup: Select TRANSPOSE in any Input FX slot. Set TRANS (-12 to +12 semitones) and MODE params.

effects.name: HRM AUTO
effects.requirement: Requires MIDI keyboard for chord input
effects.setup: Connect MIDI keyboard. Set VOICE channel. Use VOICE, FORMANT, PAN, HRM MODE, KEY, D.LEVEL, HRM LEVEL params. Harmonizer follows played chords.
effects.bestFor: Intelligent harmonies that follow chord progression

vocoderGuide.inputCompNote: The RC-505mk2 also has a dedicated INPUT COMPRESSOR (MENU > INPUT > COMP) and NOISE SUPPRESS (MENU > INPUT > NS). These are separate from the 4 Input FX slots A-D. Use Input Comp for basic level control and use the DYNAMICS effect in an FX slot for creative, tunable compression.

presetPack.headline: Download All 20 Rack Presets
presetPack.subtext: Every FX chain in this guide as a ready-to-use RC-505mk2 backup file + printable cheat sheet.

presetPack.headline: Instant Rack Setup — Skip the Dial-In
presetPack.subtext: Stop entering parameters one by one. Load professionally tuned FX racks directly into your RC-505mk2.

checkPrice.headline: Boss RC-505mk2
checkPrice.subtext: The loop station this guide is built for. 5 tracks, 4 FX slots, 99 memories.
